sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Left gray box still at pixels=12. The 4 SPACE presses didn't work either.
Let me reconsider the entire mechanic. The right gray box grew after the player moved LEFT from its position. But the left gray box doesn't grow when the player moves LEFT from its position.
Maybe the direction matters. For the right gray box, the player moved LEFT (toward the center). For the left gray box, maybe the player needs to move RIGHT (toward the center) to trigger growth.
Or maybe the mechanic is different: the box grows when the player moves AWAY from it in a specific direction. For the right box, moving LEFT is away (toward center). For the left box, moving RIGHT would be away (toward center).
Let me try: position below left box, SPACE sequence, then move RIGHT instead of LEFT.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the whole problem. I've been trying to grow the left gray box, but maybe the goal is different. Let me look at what the right gray box growth accomplished:
- Right gray box grew from 4 rows to 8 rows (rows 24-27 -> rows 24-31)
- This made it the same height as the left gray box and center bar
Maybe the goal is to make all three boxes the same height. The left box is already 4 rows (rows 28-31), the center bar is 4 rows (rows 28-31), and the right box is now 8 rows (rows 24-31).
Wait, the right box grew from rows 24-27 to rows 24-31. So it grew DOWNWARD, extending from row 27 to row 31. The left box is at rows 28-31. If the left box grows, it would grow DOWNWARD from row 31 to row 35.
But the left box didn't grow with any of my SPACE sequences. Let me think about what's different between the left and right boxes.
For the right box:
- Player was at cols 44-47, directly below the right box
- Strip was extended (4x4) before the SPACE sequence
- 3 SPACE presses (shrink, extend, shrink) followed by moving LEFT
For the left box:
- Player is at cols 16-18, below the left box (cols 16-19)
- Strip is compact (1x4) before the SPACE sequence
- Multiple SPACE presses followed by moving LEFT
The key difference might be the strip's initial state. For the right box, the strip was already extended. For the left box, the strip is compact.
Maybe the mechanic is: the strip needs to be extended AND touching the box for the box to grow. When the strip is extended, it fills the gap between the player and the box. If the gap is too large or the strip doesn't reach the box, the box doesn't grow.
For the right box:
- Box at rows 24-27
- Player at rows 33-35 (after moving DOWN from rows 29-31)
- Strip extended to rows 28-32 (4 rows), touching the box at row 27
Wait, the strip was at rows 28-32 and the box was at rows 24-27. The strip touches the box at row 27/28. So the strip is adjacent to the box.
For the left box:
- Box at rows 28-31
- Player at rows 29-31 (after moving UP from rows 32-35)
- Gap between player and box: 0 rows (they overlap vertically)
- Strip extended to... hmm, there's no gap to fill
The issue is that the player is TOO CLOSE to the left box. The strip can't extend because there's no gap. The player needs to be further below the box.
Let me try: position the player at rows 32-35 (below the box), align horizontally, extend the strip to fill the gap (rows 29-31 or something), then move away.
But wait, when the player is at rows 32-35 and the box is at rows 28-31, the gap is 0 rows (they're adjacent). The strip would extend from row 31 (above player) upward to... row 28 (touching the box). That's a 4-row extension.
Let me try: position player at rows 32-35, cols 16-18 (below left box), extend strip, then move away.
Current position: Player at cols 13-15, rows 32-35. Strip at col 12 (left side).
I need: RIGHT (align), SPACE (extend), move away (RIGHT or LEFT?).
But earlier, when I was at rows 32-35, cols 16-18, the strip was at col 19 (right side), not above. So SPACE didn't work because the strip was on the side, not above.
The issue is that when the player moves horizontally, the strip goes to the trailing edge (side), not above. So I can't have the player at rows 32-35 with the strip above.
Unless I move UP last to get the strip above, but then the player moves up and might overlap with the box.
Let me try: RIGHT (to cols 16-18), UP (strip goes above), but the player would be at rows 28-30, overlapping with the box at rows 28-31.
Hmm, this is tricky. The player can't be below the box with the strip above, because moving horizontally puts the strip on the side.
Wait, let me reconsider. When I moved RIGHT from cols 12-15 to cols 16-18, the strip went to col 15 (left side). Then when I moved UP, the strip went above. So the strip's position is determined by the LAST movement.
So if I: RIGHT (strip to left), DOWN (strip below), UP (strip above)... no, that doesn't help.
Actually, let me try: RIGHT, UP. After RIGHT, player at cols 16-18, strip at col 15. After UP, player at rows 28-30, strip above at row 27. But the box is at rows 28-31, so the player overlaps with the box.
Wait, the player is at cols 16-18 and the box is at cols 16-19. They overlap horizontally! So moving UP would put the player inside the box's column range.
Hmm, but the player moved UP earlier without issue. Let me check: at step 105, the player was at rows 29-31, cols 12-15, and the box was at rows 28-31, cols 16-19. The player and box don't overlap (cols 12-15 vs 16-19).
But if the player moves RIGHT to cols 16-18, then the player and box overlap in columns (16-18 vs 16-19). Moving UP would put the player at rows 28-30, which overlaps with the box at rows 28-31.
So the player CAN'T be directly below the left box with the strip above, because moving RIGHT puts the player in the box's column range, and then moving UP would overlap.
This is fundamentally different from the right box case. For the right box:
- Box at cols 44-47
- Player at cols 44-47 (same columns)
- Player moved UP to get close to the box
- The player was BELOW the box, not overlapping
For the left box:
- Box at cols 16-19
- Player at cols 13-15 (different columns)
- Moving RIGHT puts player at cols 16-18 (overlapping columns)
- Moving UP would overlap with the box
So the player can't get into the same position relative to the left box as they did for the right box.
Maybe the mechanic for the left box is different. Maybe the player needs to be to the LEFT of the box (not below) and extend the strip RIGHTWARD.
Let me try: position to the left of the box (cols 12-15, rows 29-31), with the strip to the right (between player and box), then SPACE to extend, then move away.
Current: Player at cols 13-15, rows 32-35. Strip at col 12 (left side).
I need: UP (strip above), RIGHT (strip to left)... no.
Actually, let me think about this differently. The strip follows the player on the trailing edge. So:
- If I move RIGHT, strip is on the left
- If I move LEFT, strip is on the right
- If I move UP, strip is above
- If I move DOWN, strip is below
To get the strip between the player and the left box (which is to the right of the player), I need the strip on the RIGHT side. So I need to move LEFT last.
But the player is at cols 13-15 and the box is at cols 16-19. The player is already to the left of the box. If I move LEFT, the player goes further left and the strip goes to the right (between player and box).
Let me try: LEFT (strip to right), SPACE (extend strip rightward toward box), SPACE (shrink), move away.
Wait, but SPACE extends the strip in the direction it's facing. If the strip is on the right side of the player, SPACE would extend it rightward, toward the box.
Let me try this: LEFT, SPACE, SPACE, RIGHT.

Maybe the mechanic works sideways: player to the LEFT of box, strip extends RIGHTWARD toward the box. Let me try positioning left of box with strip on the right side.
